steps.title: Find x and angle T using angle sum
steps.content:
In triangle $RST$, the three angles sum to $180^\circ$:
$$39 + x + (5x - 3) = 180$$
Combine like terms:
$$36 + 6x = 180 \quad \Rightarrow \quad x = 24$$
Calculate angle $T$:
$$\text{Angle } T = 5(24) - 3 = 120 - 3 = 117^\circ$$

steps.title: Apply the corresponding angles property
steps.content:
Since $\overline{LK} \parallel \overline{RT}$, the line segment $ST$ acts as a transversal cutting both parallel lines. At point $K$ on line $LK$, angle $SKL$ is formed. At point $T$ on line $RT$, angle $STR$ (the interior angle of the original triangle) is formed.

These angles occupy corresponding positions at the two intersections: both are on the same side of the transversal, measured from the parallel line to the transversal in the same rotational sense. By the Corresponding Angles Postulate, when a transversal cuts two parallel lines, corresponding angles are equal.

Therefore: $$\text{Angle } SKL = \text{Angle } T = 117^\circ$$
